Full job description
AI Data Operations Manager

Location: Remote (Applicants must be based in UK, Ireland, Greece, or Croatia)

Role Type: Full-time

Salary: Market rate / Competitive (Open to discussion based on skills and experience)

About TaskUs:

TaskUs are a provider of outsourced digital services and next-generation customer experience to fast-growing technology companies, helping its clients represent, protect and grow their brands. Leveraging a cloud-based infrastructure, TaskUs serves clients in the fastest-growing sectors, including social media, e-commerce, gaming, streaming media, food delivery, ride-sharing, HiTech, FinTech, and HealthTech.

The People First culture at TaskUs has enabled the company to expand its workforce to approximately 65,000 employees globally. Presently, we have a presence in twenty-three locations across twelve countries, which include the Philippines, India, and the United States.

The Opportunity:

You will own the day-to-day strategy and operations for human data annotation projects that power AI and ML pipelines. This includes managing geographically distributed teams, keeping workflows running smoothly, and ensuring we deliver high quality labelled data on time and to spec.

You will bring deep experience of human data operations and your understanding of what good training data looks like, and use that knowledge to shape how we work. You will partner closely with our Quality team, Solutions, Community, and client stakeholders to improve processes, raise standards, and align operations with model and client goals.

Responsibilities:

Operational Leadership

Lead end to end delivery of data annotation projects across multiple data types and use cases, ensuring agreed volumes, timelines and SLAs are met.
Plan headcount, schedules and coverage across sites and remote workers so capacity reflects forecast demand and priority work, updating plans as requirements change.
Monitor core operational metrics such as throughput, turnaround time, utilisation and rework. Flag risks early, manage incidents and escalations, coordinate corrective actions and prevent repeat issues.
Partner closely with the Quality team so that sampling plans, audits and evaluation designs are realistic and well resourced, and ensure their insights translate into improvements to workflows, coaching and guidelines.
Partner closely with the Recruitment and Training teams to ensure your team’s skillset match the client requirements.
Strategic Leadership

Work with research, engineering and other client stakeholders to translate complex project/model requirements into high-quality data pipelines.
Be the go-to expert on human data and annotation operations, providing clear guidance on best practices and helping shape how data is annotated across the team.
Use operational and quality insights to shape how the project evolves over time, refining annotation schemas, guidelines and operational workflows.
Act as a primary operational contact for clients and key internal stakeholders, owning regular business reviews, sharing key updates, and aligning on changes, risks and timelines.
Maintain clear, accurate SOPs and documentation to support operational consistency, knowledge transfer and scalability.
People Leadership

Manage a distributed team. Set clear expectations, give regular feedback and run structured performance and development conversations.
Work with Learning Experience and Quality teams to shape onboarding, calibration and upskilling plans so teams can take on new tasks and deliver to client expectations.
Create a feedback loop where team leads and annotators can surface challenges and ideas from the front line, helping continuously refine guidelines and workflows.
Required qualifications:

Bachelor’s degree in a relevant field (for example linguistics, social sciences, humanities, computer science) or equivalent practical experience.
4+ years in operations, programme or project management, with at least 2-3 years directly running data annotation, data labelling or content review operations for AI or ML products.
Proven experience in designing and owning high-quality annotation pipelines for AI/ML workflows.
Experience leading distributed or remote teams.
Strong familiarity with data annotation pipelines and how quality, sampling, audits and reviewer guidance affect performance.
Hands-on experience with annotation or labelling tools and workflows, ideally across more than one data type (for example text, images, audio or video).
Comfortable working with operational metrics and using data to understand performance, explain performance and make decisions.
Comfortable operating in a fast-moving, sometimes ambiguous environment, and able to prioritise and move work forward without perfect information.
Evidenced ability to use data and structured thinking to improve processes, not just operate them.
Strong written and verbal communication skills in English, able to communicate clearly with annotators, internal stakeholders and external clients.
Comfortable working with global, multicultural teams
Preferred qualifications:

Experience supporting data operations across the AI/ML lifecycle such as data annotation, data collection, or evaluation of models.
Background in a data provider, AI/ML company, or platform environment working in human-annotated data.
Experience working with or coordinating external vendors, freelance contributors or crowd platforms.
Working knowledge of SQL or a scripting language such as Python for deeper operational analysis and validation of metrics.
Experience helping to launch new data or annotation projects from early-stage pilots through to scaled operations.
Working understanding of AI/ML data concepts such as RLHF and SFT.
Familiarity with LLMs and using them to automate workflows.
TaskUs are the fastest growing tech-enabled business services company in the world, delivering the customer support, AI operations and content security services that power the world’s most innovative companies. Listed as one of Glassdoor‘s “100 Best Places to Work”, USA Today’s “Best Company Cultures” and “Best Companies for Women” by Comparably, TaskUs is a Frontline-First company that puts its people at the heart of everything they do. TaskUs has been recognized as one of the Inc. “5000 Fastest Growing Private Companies in America” for the past seven years consecutively. Founded in 2008 by Bryce Maddock and Jaspar Weir, the company raised over $250mm in 2018 from the world’s largest private equity firm, Blackstone.

TaskUs currently has over 65,000 employees and offices across the U.S., Philippines, India, Taiwan, Croatia and Mexico. To find out more visit.

TaskUs Inc. is an equal opportunity employer.

How We Partner To Protect You: TaskUs will neither solicit money from you during your application process nor require any form of payment in order to proceed with your application. Kindly ensure that you are always in communication with only authorized recruiters of TaskUs.

DEI: In TaskUs we believe that innovation and higher performance are brought by people from all walks of life. We welcome applicants of different backgrounds, demographics, and circumstances. Inclusive and equitable practices are our responsibility as a business. TaskUs is committed to providing equal access to opportunities. If you need reasonable accommodations in any part of the hiring process, please let us know.
Job Types: Full-time, Permanent

Benefits:

Company events
Company pension
Life insurance
Private dental insurance
Private medical insurance
Work from home
Work Location: Remote

shortRoleReason: Manages human data annotation operations for AI and ML pipelines.
companySummary: TaskUs provides outsourced digital services and next-generation customer experience to fast-growing technology companies. It serves clients across sectors including social media, e-commerce, gaming, streaming media, food delivery, ride-sharing, HiTech, FinTech, and HealthTech.
roleSummary: The role owns day-to-day strategy and operations for human data annotation projects, leading distributed teams and ensuring high-quality labelled data is delivered on time and to spec.
headlineAttraction: Own AI data operations for a global company working with leading technology clients.
rolePurpose: To lead annotation operations that produce high-quality training data for AI and ML systems while improving processes, quality, and team performance.
probablePriorities: align with client and model goals
advertSummary: TaskUs is hiring a remote AI Data Operations Manager to lead end-to-end data annotation operations for AI/ML projects. The role combines operational leadership, client/stakeholder management, quality improvement, and people leadership across distributed teams.
personalisedIntro: I’ve spent my career taking messy, high-pressure operations and making them more controlled, visible and repeatable. This role fits the way I work: clear standards, live performance management, and close attention to quality, people and delivery. I would bring that same discipline to human data operations at TaskUs.
whyThisRole: The work itself is the part that interests me most: owning day-to-day annotation operations, keeping throughput and quality under control, and tightening the link between frontline delivery and client expectations. I’ve worked in roles where performance, workflow design and team coordination all had to hold together at once, and that is the kind of environment I do well in.

Work with Recruitment and Training on team skillsets
Translate project requirements into data pipelines
Act as primary operational contact for clients and stakeholders
Shape onboarding, calibration and upskilling plans
Create feedback loops from team leads and annotators

personalisedContent.heroPositioning: Operational leader who turns complex workflows into reliable, measurable delivery.
personalisedContent.personalisedOpening: I’ve spent my career taking messy, high-pressure operations and making them more controlled, visible and repeatable. This role fits the way I work: clear standards, live performance management, and close attention to quality, people and delivery. I would bring that same discipline to human data operations at TaskUs.
personalisedContent.whyThisCompany: TaskUs stands out to me because it combines scale with a clear focus on people, and the brief shows a business built around supporting fast-moving technology clients. I also notice the global footprint and the emphasis on inclusive, frontline-first working, which matters in a role that depends on distributed teams doing careful work consistently.
personalisedContent.whyThisRole: The work itself is the part that interests me most: owning day-to-day annotation operations, keeping throughput and quality under control, and tightening the link between frontline delivery and client expectations. I’ve worked in roles where performance, workflow design and team coordination all had to hold together at once, and that is the kind of environment I do well in.

selectedEvidenceExamples.exampleTitle: Complex annual projects and team leadership
selectedEvidenceExamples.bestMatchedRoleNeed: leading distributed teams and managing operational delivery under pressure
selectedEvidenceExamples.whyChosen: I’ve led and supported a team of 12 through business-critical annual work in a high-security, data-sensitive environment, which is close to the balance this role needs between delivery control and people management.
selectedEvidenceExamples.suggestedUsage: Use as the strongest proof of structured delivery, team coordination and handling sensitive operational work.
selectedEvidenceExamples.shortLine: I’ve managed critical delivery while keeping service standards and team support in place.

selectedEvidenceExamples.bestMatchedRoleNeed: capacity planning, process improvement and operational resilience
selectedEvidenceExamples.whyChosen: I’ve worked in a scale-up environment where production discipline, capacity and resilience all needed close control. That gives me a practical base for planning coverage, improving workflows and keeping delivery stable as demand changes.
selectedEvidenceExamples.suggestedUsage: Use as a supporting example for scaling operations and improving reliability.
selectedEvidenceExamples.shortLine: I’ve helped tighten delivery discipline while the operation was growing.

selectedEvidenceExamples.exampleTitle: Re-designed triage workflow thinking for duty doctor model
selectedEvidenceExamples.bestMatchedRoleNeed: translating complex requirements into clear workflows and SOP-style guidance
selectedEvidenceExamples.whyChosen: This shows how I turn unclear operational handoffs into something staff can follow consistently. That matters in annotation work where guidelines, ownership and repeatability affect quality.
selectedEvidenceExamples.suggestedUsage: Use as a process-improvement example to show I can simplify and standardise work.
selectedEvidenceExamples.shortLine: I’ve taken unclear workflows and made them easier for teams to follow.

personalisedContent.roleNeedsSummary: My reading of this role is that it needs someone who can keep annotation delivery steady while also improving how the work is done. It is not just about managing output; it is about quality control, team rhythm, clear documentation and fast decisions when priorities change.

experienceMappings.myEvidence: I’ve managed business-critical delivery in a regulated, data-sensitive setting, balancing deadlines, service standards and team support.
experienceMappings.relevance: This shows I can hold delivery together when the work is time-sensitive and operationally important.

experienceMappings.myEvidence: I’ve led operations through a scale-up phase where capacity, process discipline and resilience all needed active management.
experienceMappings.relevance: This is relevant to planning headcount and coverage against changing demand.

experienceMappings.myEvidence: I’ve redesigned a workflow so staff had clearer ownership and fewer avoidable steps, which improved consistency in day-to-day handling.
experienceMappings.relevance: That is the same mindset needed when quality findings have to be turned into better guidance and better execution.

experienceMappings.myEvidence: I’ve supported and led a 12-person function through complex annual delivery in a structured environment.
experienceMappings.relevance: This supports the people leadership side of the role, especially where coordination and communication matter across a team.

experienceMappings.myEvidence: I’ve used sales, delivery and KPI reporting to monitor performance and strengthen execution.
experienceMappings.relevance: The role depends on reading throughput, turnaround and rework clearly, then acting on what the numbers show.

experienceMappings.myEvidence: I’ve worked in a setting where service standards, training and client-facing delivery all had to stay aligned.
experienceMappings.relevance: That fits the need to act as a primary operational contact and keep internal and external stakeholders informed.

focusAreasToBring.title: Delivery control
focusAreasToBring.summary: I would keep a close eye on volumes, turnaround, rework and risk so the operation stays predictable and issues are surfaced early.

focusAreasToBring.title: Workflow clarity
focusAreasToBring.summary: I would focus on making guidelines, SOPs and handoffs practical enough that teams can follow them consistently and improve them over time.

focusAreasToBring.title: People rhythm
focusAreasToBring.summary: I would put structure around feedback, coaching and development so team leads and annotators know what good looks like and how to get there.

personalisedContent.fitSummary: I bring a mix of operational control, process improvement and team leadership that fits the shape of this role well. My background is not in AI annotation specifically, but I have worked in environments where quality, pace, documentation and stakeholder confidence all mattered, and I know how to build order into complex work.
personalisedContent.likelyContributionSummary: Based on what the brief describes, I would expect to spend my first months getting close to the operational detail: how work is assigned, how quality is checked, where rework comes from and what slows delivery down. From there, I would look to tighten reporting, improve consistency in the workflow, and make sure the team has clearer standards, better feedback loops and more stable coverage.
companyHighlights: TaskUs is a global business with around 65,000 employees and operations across 23 locations in 12 countries.
The advert shows a strong focus on people, with a Frontline-First / People First culture.
The role sits within a business serving fast-growing technology clients across multiple sectors.
personalisedContent.cultureFitSummary: I work best in environments where the pace is high but the expectations are clear, and where people on the front line are treated as central to performance. That matches the culture signals in the brief: global, collaborative and focused on continuous improvement.

first90DaysPlan.detail: I would get close to the current annotation workflow, quality checks, reporting, team structure and stakeholder cadence so I can understand where the real pressure points are.
I would look for quick wins in coverage planning, handoffs, SOP clarity and metric visibility, while building a clearer picture of rework, risk and training needs.
I would start tightening feedback loops between delivery, quality and training, and focus on the changes that make performance more repeatable across the team.

personalisedContent.closingSummary: I would bring steady operational leadership, clear judgement and a practical approach to improving how the work runs day to day.
closingProofPoints: I improved a live NHS operation’s Google review score from 2.09 to 4.51.
I led operations through scale-up in manufacturing while improving margin and resilience.
I’ve supported a 12-person function in a regulated, data-sensitive environment.
contentNotes: I have not claimed direct AI annotation experience because it is not evidenced in the CV or shortlist.
I have used the strongest available adjacent evidence from regulated data services, workflow redesign and team-led operations.
Company highlights are limited to facts stated in the advert only.
